subject: Commonly Used Discrete Random Variables
question: A Geiger counter is monitoring the leakage of alpha particles from a container of radioactive material. Over a long period of time, an average of 50 particles per minute is measured. Assume the arrival of particles at the counter is modeled by a Poisson distribution. (a) Compute the probability that at least one particle arrives in a particular one second period. (b) Compute the probability that at least two particles arrive in a particular two second period.
